description: 24 planted flaws. 'check' = deterministic (regex-detectable) or semantic (needs judgement). Severity per the guideline catalogue. match_hints help the judge map free-text findings to flaws; a finding matches if it identifies the same defect at the same location, regardless of wording or cited rule.

flaws.description: Abstract starts with the result ('reduces the ghost-force error by two orders of magnitude') before establishing topic or problem — does not start from zero.
Abstract is technical (gradient bounds, kernel rescaling) and lacks the problem→solution arc understandable to non-specialists.
Off-storyline content: mesh generator and results-database format announced in the abstract of a coupling-scheme paper.
Introduction ends with an organization paragraph; no novelty summary ('In this paper, we introduce…') and no named state-of-the-art the contribution is positioned against.
Hard-coded cross-references ('Section 2 presents…', 'Figure 1 shows…') instead of \ref/\cref; figures carry no labels.
Undefined symbols: Psi and kappa in the blending equation are never defined; rho and b are also used before/without definition.
Long narrative of failed approaches (Lagrange multipliers, penalty method) instead of a brief remark.

Logically inaccurate: 'the material is nonlinear up to a point, the yield strength, which is a sharp kink' — a material is not nonlinear (its response is), a kink is not a point, and 'stress curve' is not a defined object.
Same quantity, two names/symbols: neighborhood radius delta (Sec. 2) reappears as 'interaction radius epsilon' (Sec. 3).
'Validation against analytical solutions' / 'we validate the implementation against the analytical solution' — comparison with analytical solutions is verification, not validation.
Convergence/error plot presented on a linear scale; should be log-log (or semi-log) to show the rate.
Duplicated word: 'the the grid spacing'.
Unquantified convergence claims: 'decreases quickly', 'converges very well', 'agreement is excellent' — no norm, no rate, no numbers.
Compared cases differ in an incidental parameter: time step 25 ns for the coarse grid but 10 ns for the finer grids, 'due to stability requirements that emerged during the study'.
Simulated and experimental crack paths plotted as two solid blue lines — indistinguishable when overlapping.
Unquantified performance claims: 'significantly faster', 'accuracy much better' — no speed-up factor, no error metric.
'in order to' (×2) instead of 'to'.
Missing hyphens in compound modifiers: 'well known problem', 'saddle point system', 'one dimensional bar'.
Title Case in the paper title ('An Efficient Coupling Of Nonlocal And Local Models…') — 'Of', 'And', 'For' capitalized.
Future-work laundry list (six unrelated directions: 3D, corrosion, fatigue, thermal, contact, lattice structures) dilutes the storyline.
Tense mixing: 'we simulate… The simulation was performed… agrees well' — unmotivated present/past switches.

Internal contradiction: conclusion claims the scheme 'avoids ghost forces by construction / compensates exactly' while the abstract claims it 'reduces the ghost-force error by two orders of magnitude' — eliminated and reduced are different claims.
Subject-verb disagreement: 'A well known problem of coupling schemes are ghost forces'.
Blending function alpha introduced, but the transition-zone width ell appears only once and kappa/Psi roles never explained; c_d defined only verbally at last use.
